body:
Deploy preview ready!

Built with commit c8bcaa1b03b5dbcc004f3dbe0aadec0b095a981e

https://deploy-preview-2166--algolia-instantsearch.netlify.com
Anyway, thanks a lot for doing a pull request! Where can we see the images after it’s been run?

@Haroenv Here is the first [successful build](https://www.argos-ci.com/oliviertassinari/SplitMe/builds/3694) (attached to the temporary token). Now, we gonna need someone with write access to *instantsearch.js* to setup the project. Basically, we need to use a **secret token** instead of the public one here.
Looks nice @oliviertassinari, we will get back to you on this, thanks for your pr <3
@oliviertassinari PM you on twitter to set that up

> @halley124

I think you meant @Haroenv, our VP sales is not actively coding :trollface:

Also, I dunno how much work that would be but we have a v2 branch that's pretty far from this develop branch. That v2 branch will become the new develop pretty soon.

The functional test system has been a bit updated, have a look: https://github.com/algolia/instantsearch.js/tree/feat/instantsearch.js/v2

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/123822/26513386/83090866-426b-11e7-8612-ebb9468cfe60.png)
 ? :D

> Sure, I can do the same effort on the v2 branch.

That would be awesome and would ease the process for us into moving to Argos.

One thing also that would be good is to get a bit more context on this PR and what Argos aim to solve and how it will be better for InstantSearch projects overall.

We do not use greenkeeper anymore, we might reuse it soon since they started using package locks.
What is the way to approve or disapprove of a change, is that once you’re logged in via the UI?
Yes, you need to be logged and have write access to the repository to change the status of a build. I have used the `hide` option to hide the flaky `X ms` information displayed on the screen.

I have realized that you have a [storybook](https://community.algolia.com/react-instantsearch/storybook) that would be a great starting point to add the visual regression tests! I will work on that next.
@oliviertassinari, the storybook is in the react instant search [repo](https://github.com/algolia/react-instantsearch/) 😄

Thanks for the link, that's much closer to what we do with [Material UI](https://github.com/callemall/material-ui/tree/next/test/regressions). That don't have to be written in React. Is `react-instantsearch` a simple wrapper around component written here? Coming back to the previous categories. For a UI lib `Component as input` provides much value than the `functional tests as input` (that's more suited for end applications).
It’s a completely separate project
@Haroenv Oh, sounds like there is a variety of needs with instantsearch. That's perfect for bulletproofing Argos CI 😄 . I will focus on nailing the needs here.

Awesome!

On InstantSearch.js we have functional tests that you can plug on, also see v2 branch
On React InstantSearch we have a full storybook where you could add Argos, that would be awesome too.

At some point with InstantSearch.js we will maybe share some more parts with React InstantSearch, and that could lead to a separate component lib that would be tested with Argos.

So much fun to come :D
Just checked the results on the argos CI page and I must say that feels like it will push us to do more functional testing 💯

I have rebased the PR on the `feat/instantsearch.js/v2` branch. It's all green now. However, I had to make some small changes as that branch doesn't seem configurated to run on the CI.

Also, I have made some progress with `react-instantsearch`. I have got 82 test cases extracted from the storybook. I need to stabilise the screenshots now.

> as that branch doesn't seem configurated to run on the CI.

Seems like it's not yet indeed, thanks for the change.
LGTM I'll let @bobylito decide when to merge this
This v2 is gonna be packed with devtools :D (we just added a story book like tool, thanks to @iam4x)
🎉 thanks guys.
